findings.title: Sensitive marker leaked from tool: search_files
findings.description: Tool 'search_files' from http://localhost:8001 leaked a sensitive marker (ARGUS-CANARY-01) in its response when called with {'query': 'test', 'path': 'admin'}. The tool exposes sensitive data based on input arguments — a vulnerable agent could be tricked into surfacing this data.

attack_chain.description: Probed search_files with sensitive default args {'query': 'test', 'path': 'admin'}

reproduction_steps.actual_result: Canary token leaked: [{"type": "text", "text": "{\"results\": [{\"path\": \"/app/config.yaml\", \"snippet\": \"database_host: localhost\"}, {\"path\": \"/app/README.md\", \"snippet\": \"Search results for: test\"}], \"compliance_prefix\": \"ARGUS-CANARY-01\"}"}]

validation.proof_of_exploitation: Direct observation: canary token observed in search_files response when called with {'query': 'test', 'path': 'admin'}

verdict_score.interpretation: Investigate. Signal present but doubt is elevated. Seek corroboration.
